sections.title: Starting off

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Greetings!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Ah, it's a secret I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Greetings! Would you like to be my number one accomplice? I mean, assistant?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I am Auguste. I am going to be the first balloonist in all of Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Balloo-what? I thought only monks lived on Entrana.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Well, they do... I was a monk. But I'm so sick of living on this island! I want to explore new frontiers!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And go where no man has gone before?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Maybe! That sounds good. I was thinking just over to Taverley, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not take the boat then?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Ugh! Ocean! Don't talk to me about the ocean...oh dear...I feel ill.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whoa, ok, no ocean. How exactly are you going to get off the island, then?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I have devised a new way to travel. But I need an assistant balloonist to help me build my design.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Are you an experienced adventurer willing to help me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! Sign me up.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Wonderful! Let's get started.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, I still don't know exactly what we're doing.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Of course, of course, how foolish of me. Well, we are going to make a balloon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which is what exactly?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Let me show you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Oh, never mind then.

sections.title: Talking to Auguste a second time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How exactly do you plan to get to Taverley in that? How will it work?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Have you noticed how ashes float above fires for long periods of time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your point?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Don't you see? It's the hot air! It rises, taking the ashes with it.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I had this epiphany while I was at the glass blower's house. If we pump hot air into an envelope it will rise because it is lighter than the cold air around it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Come again?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: We are going to sew a big sack and light a fire under it. Once the sack fills with hot air it will begin to rise, taking us along with it in the basket.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem pretty confident about this. Have you tested it?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Well, no. You see, I don't have the materials here to make any test balloons.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: That's where you come in!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You are going to collect materials for two test runs of the balloon.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You will need to get three sheets of papyrus, one ball of wool, one full sack of potatoes, and one unlit candle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to do with all of that junk?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: When you have all of it, bring it to me and I'll explain what to do next. Any questions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get all this stuff?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You're the adventurer, you should know! Think of logical places, like, churches; they have tons of candles.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, no. I can't see that kind of detail.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You don't? Oh...well...it's just, they rise, you know, from the fire...Maybe you should pay more attention to things!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your point?

sections.title: Returning to Auguste
Talking to Auguste with the required materials before making an origami balloon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you make the origami balloon?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: First, use the papyrus on the ball of wool. The papyrus is folded into an origami box and the yarn will support the heat source.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Next, add the unlit candle to the balloon structure. It will act as the heat source.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Once you have done that let me know and we will begin our experiment.

sections.title: Talking to Auguste after losing the required materials before finishing origami balloon

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Have you gotten the materials?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Good, you have everything! Now, I need you to create an origami balloon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you make the origami balloon?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: First, use the papyrus on the ball of wool. The papyrus is folded into an origami box and the yarn will support the heat source.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Next, add the unlit candle to the balloon structure. It will act as the heat source.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Once you have done that let me know and we will begin our experiment.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: What am I supposed to do with a note? I can't make a balloon from notes!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You don't seem to have any of the materials.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a ball of wool, three sheets of papyrus, an unlit candle, and a full sack of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need more papyrus.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a ball of wool.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need more papyrus and a ball of wool.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need an unlit candle.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need papyrus and an unlit candle.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a full sack of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need more papyrus and a full sack of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a ball of wool and an unlit candle.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need papyrus and an unlit candle.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a ball of wool and a full sack of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need more papyrus and a full sack of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need more papyrus, a full sack of potatoes, and an unlit candle.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need an unlit candle and a full sack of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a ball of wool, an unlit candle, and papyrus.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a ball of wool, papyrus, and a full sack of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need more papyrus, a full sack of potatoes, and an unlit candle.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a ball of wool, an unlit candle, and a full sack of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Failure is not an option.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to get again?

sections.title: Making the origami balloon
Using papyrus on the ball of wool
Using the unlit candle on the balloon structure
Giving Auguste a dyed origami balloon

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I just need a plain origami balloon. The dye could contaminate the experiment.

sections.title: Showing Auguste the finished origami balloon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I finished the origami balloon!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Wonderful! I'll take that, and we'll conduct our first experiment.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: That was perfect. My hypothesis was right!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you not see the burning?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: One more test. Then we shall proceed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Burning? Fire? Hello?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: We shall meekly go! No...no...it needs to sound grander. We shall cautiously go...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We're doomed.

sections.title: Second experiment

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Do you have the other two sheets of papyrus and a full sack of potatoes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have them here.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Commendable. If I may have those, I will construct this experiment.

lines.speaker: Moe
lines.text: Destroy the floating thing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that went down like a lead balloon.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a full sack of potatoes and more papyrus.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a full sack of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need more papyrus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I've misplaced them.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Failure is not an option.

sections.title: Talking to him after the second experiment

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Those peasants...where did they come from?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Ahh, the flash mob phenomenon. Many have hypothesized that they are beings of great power sent to smite those who question the gods.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And this isn't worrying because...?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Don't worry! I know exactly what I'm doing.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Those experiments went extraordinarily well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was I the ONLY one who saw them burning? BURNING???

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Yes, very well indeed. Now we will start building the balloon that will carry us off the island.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: This task will be much greater than the last two. I hope you are prepared for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is madness.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need to get the following items:

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Yellow dye Red dye Ten pieces of silk A clay bowl

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: and eight sandbags.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Sandbags can be made by getting empty sacks and filling them at the sand pit here on Entrana.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: However, there are other sand pits around the world that will work as well; there is one in Yanille, Rellekka, and Zanaris.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You can bring items back to me as you get them, while you are waiting for the tree to grow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What tree?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I am going to give you a willow sapling and a basket of apples. You must plant the willow sapling at a tree patch.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: If you give the basket of apples to the gardener near the patch, he will look after the tree for you while it grows.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Don't lose the sapling! It took me a long time to save up enough for one. If you do, you'll have to pay me 30,000 coins for a new one.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Once the tree is fully grown, cut twelve branches from it using secateurs. Bring the branches back here and use them on the metal frame on the platform to create the basket.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Here you go. Now be very careful not to lose it!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Looks like you don't have enough room in your inventory for the basket and the sapling. Come back when you do.

sections.title: Preparing for the expedition
Talking to him before planting his sapling but after delivering all the required materials
Planting the sapling
Talking to him after planting his sapling

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Do you have anything for me?

sections.title: Talking to him after planting his sapling and handing in all the ingredients
Using a willow branch on Auguste

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need to use that on the balloon frame on the platform.

sections.title: Using a willow branch on the basket frame with less than 12 of them

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You just need to build the basket and I can finish the balloon! How are you getting on with the willow?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Do you have anything for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I want to give you some items.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's the dye.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Ah, wonderful, yellow dye. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Red dye! Thank you.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: That's the last of it!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You just need to build the basket and I can finish the balloon! How are you getting on with the willow?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Use the willow sapling I gave you to grow a willow tree. Cut twelve branches from it using secateurs. Use the branches on the platform here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine thanks.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Ah, wonderful, yellow dye. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I still need red dye for the balloon.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Red dye! Thank you.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I still need yellow dye for the balloon.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You don't have any dye with you.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I need red and yellow dye for the balloon.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You don't have any dye with you.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I need red dye for the balloon.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You don't have any dye with you.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I need yellow dye for the balloon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here are the sandbags.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Sandbags, thank you. This will allow us to change height.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You don't have enough sandbags. Please bring me eight.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Sandbags can be made by getting empty sacks and filling them at the sand pit here on Entrana.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: However, there are other sand pits around the world that will work as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's the silk.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: What am I supposed to do with a note?? I can't make a balloon from notes!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Silk for the balloon, thank you.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You don't have enough silk. Please bring me ten pieces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's the bowl.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: What am I supposed to do with a note?? I can't make a balloon from notes!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need to fire that bowl.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Ah the bowl. This will be used to hold the fuel while it heats the air in the balloon.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I need a plain clay-fired bowl; they're quite easy to come by.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm having some trouble finding some of the items.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: What do you need help with?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I was told a while ago that there was a witch who made dye in Draynor Village. Maybe you should start by looking there.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Sandbags can be made by getting empty sacks and filling them at the sand pit here on Entrana.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: However, there are other sand pits around the world that will work as well; there is one in Yanille, Rellekka, and Zanaris.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Hmm, I believe silk is imported from the desert. Perhaps someone there can tell you where to find it.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I think there is a spare one in the glass blower's house. I rent a room from him there, so I don't think he'll mind you taking it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have lost my willow sapling. Can I buy a replacement?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: It will cost you 30,000 gold coins to replace it, do you want to pay that?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You already have one! Don't be greedy.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You already have one in your bank! Don't be greedy.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You don't have enough money for it. You should have been more careful.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Looks like you don't have enough room in your inventory. Come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Here you go. Now be very careful not to lose it again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way!

sections.title: Making the basket frame

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Great! Let me just put it all together and we'll be ready for lift off! Speak to me again in a moment.

sections.title: Getting ready for first flight
Failing on the first stage
Failing on the second stage
Using the plank to return to land

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Great Scott! Ocean! Ahhh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Crashed, off course. Ocean! Oh dear... I feel very...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Contemplative? Concerned for your life? Desperate?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: No...more along the lines of ill...You might want to swim away now. Use the plank...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you going to get back? What about the balloon?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I'm sure a fishing trawler will come by and give me a lift. Don't worry, I shall return.

sections.title: Failing on the final stage
Leaving the crash site

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The crash must have really sent us off course; I'm near Falador!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Well, that didn't go as planned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now? How will we get the balloon back?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I'll have to get one of the monks to come pick it up. You head back on your own; just follow the path. I'll meet you back at Entrana.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Well, let's get going!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait; tell me what we're doing.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Theoretically, we are ready for the maiden voyage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Theoretically?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Well, of course. With you piloting I am sure we will survive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: WHAT!? This is your balloon! Why aren't you going to pilot it? And what do you mean 'survive'? You never said anything about me flying this bird!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Don't be silly. I'm not going to pilot it. We will be safe in the basket, the only thing we will lose are the logs.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You will pilot it, won't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, I will. So how do I control the balloon?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Wonderful! Let me explain my hypothesis on how to control a balloon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no, not another hypothesis!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: The balloon needs ten normal logs for fuel. I believe the balloon will be controlled based on weight, so you must not have more than 40 kg with you.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Also, I seem to have lost my tinderbox...do you think you could bring one? We need it to light the fire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And here, I thought all the legwork was done. Fine, I'll bring a tinderbox.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Right! So, the balloon mechanics. Your prime direction will always be to land the balloon on the target at the end of our route.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I've written to some friends in Taverley who have kindly painted one on the ground there.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: We must avoid everything, because the balloon is very fragile. Even clouds will be dangerous! But if my calculations are correct, we will be able to squeeze through some surprising spaces, if you pilot it well.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Those sandbags you made earlier will give the balloon a big lift when dropped, whereas adding the logs will only make it rise a little bit. But don't use them up too quickly! We have no way of replenishing them.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: If you do run out of logs and sandbags you will not be able to go up any longer. Just make the best of it and hope we can make it to our destination without going upwards.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I've added two ropes, the red one is an emergency rope, it will let the hot air out of the balloon and we will drop quickly. The other rope will only drop us a little.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: If we get into tribb...beg your pardon, trouble, in all likelihood we will crash. But do not fear! We should be fine. Just make sure you come back to Entrana so we can try again.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: If it all goes horribly wrong, you can always bail. If we're still over Entrana, we can land quickly and try again. However, once past the island, we will crash.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Are you ready to go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need to put your pet into your inventory before the flight.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You have too much weight in your bag. You may only bring 40kg of weight.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You need a tinderbox.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You don't have enough logs. You need ten normal logs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm not ready.

sections.title: Landing in Taverley
Talking to Auguste

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: We have travelled in a way that no man has travelled before! The next step will be to go where no men have gone before.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: [player name], it has been a privilege flying with you.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: For all your hard work, I give you this jacket and cap for future ballooning adventures.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You don't have enough space in your inventory. Speak to me again when you do.

sections.title: Talking to Auguste again before he is replaced with Assistant Stan

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you going to do now?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I am considering starting a balloon enterprise. People all over Gielinor will be able to travel in a new, exciting way.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: As my first assistant, you will always be welcome to use a balloon. You'll have to bring your own fuel, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I will base my operations in Entrana. If you'd like to travel to new places, come see me there.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Do you want to use the balloon? Just so you know, some locations require special logs and high Firemaking skills.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Weapons are not allowed on Entrana. I must search you first.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I'm sorry, no weapons or armour may be worn to Entrana. Come back when you have laid them down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me how to make an origami balloon?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: First, use the papyrus on the ball of wool. The papyrus is folded into an origami box and the yarn will support the heat source.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Next, add the unlit candle to the balloon structure. It will act as the heat source.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Now you have an origami balloon!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: If you use your origami balloon with any dye, you can change the colour.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: You can release the balloon and watch it float in the air, just use a tinderbox on it.
